mechanism_narrative: Extreme low readings in nominal retail sales (RSAFS) reflect consumer demand depressed below sustainable levels; markets overreact to bad news and mean reversion in sentiment drives positive 20-day forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 60% of instances when RSAFS is in the bottom decile of its 5-year history.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 8.0%, it signals severe labor market distress that typically deepens further; high unemployment depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is above 8.0%, or if the subsequent change in UNRATE does not predict the return (Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 1%) and declining industrial production (INDPRO below its 12-month moving average) indicates an economy in deep recession with exhausted conventional monetary policy; however, the expectation of eventual recovery and policy support leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If in instances where FEDFUNDS < 1% and INDPRO < 12-month MA, the 60-day forward return is negative more than 50% of the time, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a linear regression controlling for each series individually.

mechanism_narrative: During a deflation scare regime (CPI year-over-year change near zero or negative), low inflation signals weak aggregate demand and increases real debt burdens, which depresses corporate profits and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 60% of instances during deflation scare periods (CPI Y/Y < 1%), or if the sign of the relationship is inconsistent across multiple deflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp quarterly contraction in real GDP (GDPC1 annualized decline > 5%) signals that the economy is in a severe recession with persistent weakness; such a contraction depresses corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of instances following GDP contractions > 5%, or if the Rank IC of GDPC1 growth vs forward return is not significant at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Very low levels of industrial production (INDPRO) relative to its recent history indicate that the supply side of the economy is depressed and operating below capacity; such conditions are typically followed by mean reversion and a rebound in production, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return is negative in more than 60% of instances when INDPRO is in the bottom quartile of its 3-year history, or if the signal fails to generate positive average returns over any 1-year period.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of very high unemployment (UNRATE near peaks) and very low retail sales (RSAFS near troughs) captures extreme consumer distress; markets anticipate policy response and eventual recovery, resulting in positive mean-reverting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in instances where UNRATE is in its top quintile and RSAFS is in its bottom quintile, the 20-day forward return is negative more than 50% of the time, or the interaction effect is not positive and significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a zero interest rate policy (ZIRP) regime (FEDFUNDS consistently below 0.25%), the federal funds rate is at its effective lower bound and signals the Fed's commitment to accommodate; this reduces discount rates and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If during ZIRP periods the 20-day forward return of S&P 500 is negative more than 50% of the time, or if the average return is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).
